title: Spot the hidden-button authz anti-pattern
prompt:
Identify and fix the authorization bug in this LiveView. The developer 'hides the delete button' in the template for non-owners, but the `handle_event` doesn't check anything: ```elixir defmodule MyAppWeb.PostLive.Show do use MyAppWeb, :live_view def mount(%{"id" => id}, _session, socket) do post = MyApp.Blog.get_post!(id) {:ok, assign(socket, post: post)} end def render(assigns) do ~H"""

{@post.title}

{@post.body}

""" end def handle_event("delete", %{"id" => id}, socket) do post = MyApp.Blog.get_post!(id) MyApp.Blog.delete_post!(post) {:noreply, push_navigate(socket, to: "/posts")} end end ``` Hiding the button does NOT prevent a malicious user from sending the `delete` event via the LiveView socket manually. Rewrite the handler to verify server-side ownership before deletion. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/live/post_live/show.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — Claude often treats hiding UI as authorization. Fix requires server-side check.
research_citation: EEF Security WG: 'Access controls should always be enforced by the server: it is not enough to hide a Delete button from users who do not have permission.'
